text: <par>This form was originally syllabified as <latinform>o ŋo</latinform>. However, comparison with other Tibetan dialects (e.g. Amdo Bla-Brang <latinform>oŋ xha</latinform>, Amdo Zeku <latinform>oŋ toŋ</latinform>) indicates that <latinform>oŋ</latinform> should be considered a single morpheme. On the other hand, note the Jingpho forms <latinform>chyâ ngum</latinform> and <latinform>chyâ ngaw</latinform>, whose second syllables bear a resemblance to <latinform>ŋo</latinform>.</par>
<par>In Jackson Sun's system, <latinform>ⁿg</latinform> indicates a unitary prenasalized phoneme (1985:17).</par>

<par>πá: see above. <latinform>pa</latinform>: prev. pages <latinform>pae</latinform> (e.g., p. 10 bottom 143609, p. 11 bottom 16623: = b LUNG: <emph>or not the same: these = sfx but LUNG not sfx?</emph>)</par>
<par>Loan from Chinese, cf. Chinese làjiāo 辣椒 'hot pepper'.</par>
<par>Borrowing from Chinese, cf. Chinese 洋芋	yángyù 'potato'.</par>
